title: Lattice Realization of Twist Defects in a $\mathbb{Z}_2\times \mathbb{Z}_2$ Topological Order
abstract: In this work, we explore a microscopic realization of three types of anyonic symmetries in a $\mathbb{Z}_2\times\mathbb{Z}_2$ topological order, corresponding to a double toric code. These symmetries act as nontrivial permutations on the anyon labels of the parent state. We consider a setup consisting of two decoupled Wen plaquette models stacked on top of each other and introduce dislocations that modify the Hamiltonian, giving rise to localized twist defects, eventually inducing interactions between the layers. In this context, branch cuts act as sources of anyon permutations when they cross it. We characterize the defects by calculating their quantum dimensions, and we also consider double loop operators around them that allow us to determine the non-Abelian fusion rules between the defects, including when they carry different anyon permutations.
summary: This paper provides a microscopic lattice construction for twist defects in a bilayer topological system. It shows how lattice dislocations can be used to realize anyonic symmetries and how these defects interact through non-Abelian fusion rules. This work enriches the understanding of how topological orders can be manipulated and interconnected via lattice-level defects.
main_problem: The paper explores the microscopic lattice realization of anyonic symmetries and their associated twist defects within a Z2 x Z2 topological order.
main_result: The study demonstrates that lattice dislocations can induce twist defects that permute anyon labels, establishes non-Abelian fusion rules for these defects, and shows that the fusion of electric-magnetic duality and layer exchange symmetries results in a trivalent junction carrying an Abelian anyon.
method: The authors use an algebraic approach involving the analysis of loop (Wilson) operators, K-matrix Chern-Simons theory, and the construction of branch cuts and dislocations in a lattice Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: A Z2 x Z2 topological order constructed as a bilayer of two decoupled Wen plaquette models (a double toric code) on a square lattice.
key_observables: Quantum dimensions, fusion rules, double loop operator eigenvalues, and ground state degeneracy.
important_parameters: The anyon labels in Z2^4, the K-matrix, and the specific permutations (sigma, rho, xi) of the anyonic symmetries.
main_assumptions: The derivation assumes the validity of the K-matrix formalism and the specific structure of the Z2 x Z2 topological order.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates a dislocation defect and anyon mutation; Figure 2 shows closed loops of e or m particles; Figure 5 depicts a branch cut with pentagon operators; Figure 6 depicts the fusion of sigma and rho defects at a trivalent junction.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the microscopic model, defines the anyonic symmetries, details the lattice implementation of twist defects via dislocations, performs algebraic characterization of defect types and fusion rules, and concludes with a detailed derivation of loop operator eigenvalues.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
